acceptance_notes: Guarded patch mode: preserving stable base schema while adding narrow evidence-backed refinements
Positive transfer evidence (dapt-intrusion-detection 0→100, earthquake-phase-association 100 score) validates core pipeline emphasis
Protected regressions (azure-bgp-oscillation-route-leak, exceltable-in-ppt both 100→0) trigger explicit guardrails against overapplication
Six zero-margin borderline cases with artifact-generation require sharper boundary articulation
One assigned task (court-form-filling) has artifact-generation seed but 60 score, indicating boundary ambiguity
Patch adds workflow-assumption explicitness and differentiation criteria without removing working incumbent items

challenger_guidance.conservative: Keep analytic-pipeline when the task explicitly requires staged processing (extract → transform → analyze → report) with intermediate validation gates, and when later stage correctness depends on earlier stage artifact quality.
challenger_guidance.differentiating: Choose analytic-pipeline over artifact-generation when intermediate processing quality strongly affects final correctness (e.g., seismic phase picking quality determines event association F1 score). Choose artifact-generation when the task succeeds by meeting strict output contract constraints in a single generation step, even if conceptually multi-phase.
challenger_guidance.exploratory: Try analytic-pipeline when the task shows pipeline vocabulary (phases, stages, workflow) or involves ordered transformations with measurement/analysis steps, but be prepared to yield to artifact-generation if the task succeeds via single-artifact output discipline rather than stage decomposition.

expected_effect: Sharper task-routing boundary with artifact-generation schema, reducing zero-margin borderline ambiguity
Preserved high performance on core pipeline tasks (dapt-intrusion-detection, earthquake-phase-association patterns)
Explicit workflow-topology assumption reduces misapplication to single-artifact tasks
Named handoff requirements make schema differentiation criteria more testable
Protected regression guardrails prevent future overapplication to non-pipeline patterns

add: Explicit staged-multi-step workflow assumption in emphasize to match successful task profiles
Named execution/verification handoffs to make tool use less implicit
Differentiation criteria vs artifact-generation for borderline task routing
Task-specific failure mode extraction before generic scaffolding
Guardrails against schema broadening that regresses stable prior behavior
Expected_bad_fit expansion for artifact-generation boundary cases
Hypothesized failure modes covering boundary ambiguity and protected regressions
exclude: Any broadening based on regression evidence (guarded_patch constraint)
Removal of working incumbent items without direct causation evidence
Generic procedural additions not grounded in positive transfer pairs
keep: All current emphasize items (stage decomposition, intermediate artifacts, handoff integrity, pipeline evidence) - validated by positive transfer
All current avoid items (collapsing transforms, skipping validation, generic prose) - still relevant
All current expected_good_fit and expected_bad_fit items - baseline boundaries remain valid
Core semantic_intent and meta_prompt - working on true pipeline tasks

add: applying pipeline framing to tasks where the dominant pattern is single-artifact generation with review
schema broadening that regresses previously stable task-schema pairings
assume a staged-multi-step workflow topology; make stage boundaries explicit before rendering task-specific guidance
name the required execution handoffs (data extraction → transformation → analysis) and verification handoffs (intermediate validation gates) instead of leaving tool orchestration implicit
extract the task-specific failure mode or constraint before layering broader procedural scaffolding
on borderline cases with artifact-generation, articulate why intermediate processing quality affects final correctness in this task
single-step artifact generation tasks where review discipline matters more than stage decomposition
borderline cases where artifact-generation schema wins because output contract constraints dominate over intermediate processing quality
tasks with deterministic-output-contract verifier mode when staged workflow topology matches the task structure
tasks whose task_profile primary_pattern is pipeline
tool-heavy workflows where intermediate artifact quality gates prevent downstream error propagation
low-margin schema-boundary ambiguity with artifact-generation on tasks that superficially appear multi-stage but succeed via single-artifact discipline
outer-loop candidate regressions on previously stable tasks when pipeline emphasis is overapplied to non-pipeline patterns
